body: Documents registered in document-registry.jsonl must carry last_verified and stale_after_days fields. Without freshness metadata, 113 of 168 docs cannot be audited for staleness. Every new doc registered must include both fields. Existing critical docs (source_of_truth/current_status) should be backfilled.

Receipt documents must not claim 'Ruff clean' globally when pre-existing Ruff debt (excluded files) exists. Found 2 instances in agent-output-contract.md and verification-debt-policy.md. All future receipts must qualify Ruff claims with explicit scope.

Agentic pattern detection found 26 violations across phase closure docs. CandidateRule promotion language, READY-as-authorization patterns, and C4 gate mismatches persist in docs written before ADP-3 was activated. These are pre-existing doc debt that should be cleaned up incrementally.

Finance boundary checker found 87 live trading references across docs. Most are in boundary-definition documents that describe (not violate) the boundary. However, 15+ instances are in operational documents that should be cleaned. Safe context detection needs improvement — definitional docs should be excluded, not just negated.

Protected paths checker found 285 unprotected references to .env, secrets, pyproject.toml across docs. Most are governance docs describing the protection boundary itself. Protected path checker needs safe-file exclusions similar to agentic-patterns checker.

Hardcoded derived counts (e.g., '244 registered docs') in 4 documents drifted from the actual registry count (435) after a batch of 124 new documents was added. The root cause: 4 documents independently stored the same 'fact' (DG entry count) rather than referencing a single source of truth. This is a systemic pattern: any numeric fact that appears in multiple places WILL drift. The fix: replace hardcoded counts with generated_view references derived from document-registry.jsonl. Top projects (K8s hack/update+verify, Protobuf code-gen, Rust tidy) enforce this with generated-file drift detection in CI. Rule candidate: no document may hardcode a count derivable from a machine-readable source. All such counts must be generated views or explicitly marked as potentially stale snapshots.

VALID_DOC_TYPES was maintained in TWO locations: scripts/check_document_registry.py (20 types) and checkers/document-registry/run.py (17 types). They were out of sync. Adding 4 new doc_types (boundary, inventory, supporting_evidence, triage) required editing both files. This is the dual-checker trap: any set/list/schema maintained in two places WILL diverge. The systemic fix: extract to a single schema file (document-types.json) and have both checkers load from it. This mirrors Protobuf's schema-first architecture, K8s CRD code-generators, and GraphQL's single-schema pattern. Rule candidate: no governance constant, enumeration, or type list may exist in more than one file. Single schema, multiple consumers, CI-enforced consistency.

A batch of 124 new documents was committed across 11 atomic batches (SRC-CORE, TESTS, DOCS-AI, DOCS-DGP, DOCS-LGC-01/02, DOCS-RG, DOCS-LEDGERS, DOCS-SUMMITS, DOCS-ARCHIVE, GITIGNORE), but the governance system's self-referential checks (current-truth-protocol, document-registry, agentic-patterns, protected-paths) were not kept in sync within the same commits. Result: CI was BLOCKED for 4 consecutive pushes. The systemic issue: adding a document requires a minimum atomic operation: (1) file creation, (2) registry entry, (3) doc_type registration (if new), (4) generated view update, (5) reference doc updates. This mirrors Google monorepo's per-commit self-consistency, Linux's 'every commit must compile', and K8s KEP's single-PR requirement. Rule candidate: any operation that changes governed reality must include the governance system updates in the same commit. CI must verify self-consistency atomically.

When the governance system detects its own drift (BLOCKED → systematic A1-A4 fix → READY), the event itself is a methodology source. The CI Self-Calibration of 2026-05-10 produced: (1) 12 L0 invariants organized into Epistemic and Authority groups; (2) a dual-gate governance loop replacing the single-gate model; (3) formalization of the A1-A4 disposition matrix; (4) the self-calibration doctrine (governance system is governable, self-auditing but not self-proving); (5) 6 anti-patterns with root causes and fixes; (6) the receipt standard requiring git diff + before/after counts + raw CLI output. Rule candidate: every governance self-calibration event that produces methodology MUST result in a methodology document registered at L0. The methodology must include its own red-team boundaries — what it does NOT establish, where it could fail.
